qa_pairs.question: What does `<path-to-local-file-image>` refer to when uploading an image to OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: `<path-to-local-file-image>` refers to the path on your local machine to the image file you want to upload.

qa_pairs.question: What does the `<format>` parameter specify during an image upload to OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: The `<format>` parameter specifies the disk format of the image being uploaded.

qa_pairs.question: What disk format is assumed if you don't specify the `<format>` parameter when uploading an image?
qa_pairs.answer: If the disk format is not specified, the raw format is assumed.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to specify the correct disk format if your image is not raw?
qa_pairs.answer: If your image is not in raw format, you must choose a format that matches your image's actual format; otherwise, it will cause issues.

qa_pairs.question: What does `<new-image-name>` represent when uploading an image to OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: `<new-image-name>` is the name that the image will have when it appears on the OpenStack dashboard.
